title: Process
description: A Process is a Standard Operating Procedure: a named, owned, ordered collection of Steps that accomplishes a business goal. Each Step references a Task. Processes are purely descriptive in this version — they document the human playbook behind a set of Capability executions. A Process's operator is a Role responsible for the workflow; the actual work is performed by whichever Roles each Task names. Runtime orchestration semantics (state tracking, timers, SLAs) are deferred to the planned workflow-cell.

name.description: The PascalCase name of the process.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this process accomplishes.

domain.description: The dot-separated domain path this process is scoped to.

operator.description: The Role (PascalCase) responsible for owning and supervising this process. Must reference a declared Role.

steps.description: Ordered collection of Steps. Step IDs are referenced by depends_on to form a DAG. A Step with no depends_on is a root (may run at the start of the process).

id.description: The kebab-case identifier of this step, unique within the process. Used as the target for depends_on references.

task.description: The Task name this step executes. Must reference a declared Task.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this step accomplishes within the process.

depends_on.description: Sibling step IDs that must complete before this step begins. Empty or missing means the step is a root. Multiple entries mean the step waits on all predecessors (AND semantics) — this is how parallel branches fan in.

branch.description: Optional conditional gate on this step. If 'when' evaluates false, the step is skipped and control passes to 'else' (either a sibling step ID or the literal 'abort').

when.description: A human-readable condition expression. The evaluation engine is defined by the workflow-cell; for now this is documentation.

else.description: Either a sibling step ID to jump to when 'when' is false, or the literal 'abort' to end the process.

emits.description: Optional Signals emitted when the terminal steps of this process complete. Signal names must reference declared Signals.

examples.name: LoanOrigination
examples.description: End-to-end loan origination from application to disbursement.
